# Questions in Livestream 223

Live Stream #223: A Question of Assessed Damage

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=470" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:50</a> | How do you deal with Luke's Quirinius census problem? | He treats Luke's wording as difficult but not fatal, and suggests it likely reflects a broader census process tied to Quirinius rather than a simple blunder. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=762" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:42</a> | Could Quirinius have handled the census in an earlier role, before the governorship we know best? | He thinks that is possible because Quirinius may have held earlier authority in Syria before the later governorship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=867" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">14:27</a> | Have we found the tomb of Herod the Great? | He says the Herodium identification is probably right, though not every detail is beyond dispute. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:29</a> | What do you think about Creation Ministries International running an Egypt tour? | He is skeptical and says he would rather travel with a stronger archaeologist or guide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1118" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:38</a> | Is CMI as bad as ABR? | He says it is not identical in every respect, but he still treats CMI as unreliable on archaeology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1206" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:06</a> | On the Seti I Karnak map, could the two structures be Pi-Ramesses and Avaris, with other forts now missing? | He thinks that reconstruction is possible, but the damaged map and missing stations keep it uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:45</a> | What is so bad about a spindle in 2 Samuel 3:29? | He reads it as a curse of humiliation and low-status labor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1706" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:26</a> | Was Joab David's army commander, or a different Joab? | He says this is Joab son of Zeruiah, David's commander. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:41</a> | What do you think of Liverani's conclusion that Judah coexisted during Saul's kingdom? | He thinks Liverani overstates things; Judah likely existed, though the early monarchy was still small and loosely organized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=1992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:12</a> | Can you explain how bakshish works? | He describes it as anything from an ordinary tip to a grease payment, depending on context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=2379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:39</a> | Is Matthew 7:7-11 a description or a promise? | He treats it as both invitation and promise, but not as a blank-check guarantee for whatever people want. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=2543" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:23</a> | Is the death count in Numbers 16:49 idiomatic or literal? | He says either an idiomatic or literal reading could be possible, and the passage is hard to settle. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=2684" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:44</a> | Could that number be clans rather than thousands? | He says reading the Hebrew as clans and family groups is a possible contextual reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=2834" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:14</a> | What does a heave offering look like in practice, and is it the same as a wave offering? | He says it is something lifted or set apart for priestly use, while the exact wave-heave distinction can blur by passage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=3263" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:23</a> | Why does Exodus 18:21 mention hundreds, fifties, and tens? | He treats them as nested units for delegated authority and administration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=3427" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:07</a> | What facts would strengthen an inductive argument for the uniqueness of the Exodus? | He says the strongest case comes from converging chronology, settlement evidence, Egyptian context, and Israel's unusual memory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=3556" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:16</a> | What do you think of Zach Miller's video about you and Hebrew? | He dismisses it as uninformed and says it misreads both Hebrew and his position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=3788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:08</a> | Why did India and Greece develop formal logic while the ancient Near East did not? | He ties formal logic to those later intellectual traditions and says the ancient Near East stayed more practical and rhetorical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=3992" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:32</a> | Have you read the *Masked Man of Cairo* series? | He has not read it and cannot say much about it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=4094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:14</a> | How does Jerusalem appear in ancient records? | He says the earliest records use a form like Uru-Shalem in the Amarna letters, while Jebus may have been an Israelite ethnic toponym. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=4210" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:10</a> | Is `uru` Sumerian or Akkadian? | He answers Akkadian in this context, because the Amarna letters are written in Akkadian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=4538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:38</a> | What pins Job to the Iron Age rather than patriarchal times, and who would be its contemporaries? | He places Job later than the patriarchs on language and world-setting grounds, roughly in an Iron Age wisdom context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=4797" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:57</a> | Did any Egyptian gods have a sarcophagus? | He says Osiris was said in the myth to have been mummified and buried in a sarcophagus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=5037" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:57</a> | Did Osiris meet Sokar? | He treats it as a question about the merger of Osiris and Sokar traditions, where the Osiris cult absorbed Sokar's ritual practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=5068" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:28</a> | Do you know much about Neithhotep and Narmer's wife's tomb? | He says he knows very little about it and would need to look it up. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=5380" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:40</a> | Why did Egyptians never develop the concept of reincarnation of the soul? | He says the idea was mainly associated with the smaller Sokar cult, which was later absorbed by the stronger Osiris cult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=5496" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:36</a> | Is there anything buried beneath the Sphinx? | He says there is no clear evidence for major chambers or treasures underneath it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=5662" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:22</a> | Was there political or religious turmoil in Egypt under Ramesses I, Seti I, or Ramesses II? | He says there was some transition and tension, but not sweeping collapse under those rulers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=6089" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:29</a> | Was Ankhsenamun like the character in *The Mummy*? | He says the movie used the name from the real Egyptian figure, King Tut's wife, though not the movie storyline. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=6200" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:20</a> | What is the worst pastry you ever had? | He answers with a personal food anecdote rather than a technical argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=7024" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:04</a> | What makes you hopeful about influencing culture if the marketing campaign is basically nonexistent? | He says readers, recommendations, reviews, and back-catalog discovery are what actually move books without a publisher marketing budget. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=7358" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:38</a> | What science-fiction writer do you like most, or who are you most like? | He names favorite writers and says he is more a blend than a copy of any one of them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=7837" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:37</a> | Do Christians underestimate the value of what a promise is to God? | He says yes, and argues that modern readers often miss the covenant weight of vow and oath language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=7910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:50</a> | Why was God angry at Balaam in Numbers 22:22? | He says Balaam's corrupt intention was already wrong, so the angel confronts more than outward obedience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=8501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:41</a> | Why is it difficult for people to understand their thirst for spirituality? | He says people redirect spiritual hunger into substitutes and often resist the true object of that hunger. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=8904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:24</a> | What do you think of the Ezra 2 and Nehemiah 7 census discrepancy and the claim that archaeology does not support the return from exile? | He says the number issue has ordinary textual or administrative explanations, and he rejects the claim that archaeology disproves the return. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=9206" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:26</a> | Did Project SETI ever find evidence of aliens? | He says no credible evidence has come from it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=9336" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:36</a> | Is it true the early church fathers all believed Mary was a perpetual virgin? | He says no, argues many did not treat it as a core doctrine, and points to Matthew 1:25 as a counterargument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=9480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:00</a> | Are there any records outside the Bible hinting at Nebuchadnezzar's seven-year madness? | He says no for Nebuchadnezzar, though some Babylonian material has been connected instead with Nabonidus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=9765" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:45</a> | What besides food practices and housing helps show the Exodus was a unique event? | He points to chronology, geography, settlement shifts, and Israel's distinct historical memory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=10250" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:50</a> | Was Jonah's great fish Leviathan, and if it was a sea creature, what could it have been? | He doubts the text requires Leviathan and says it only points to a great sea creature, with the exact species uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=10409" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:29</a> | If Noah's flood were global, how long ago would it need to be for archaeology? | He says a global model would have to push the event very far back to fit the archaeological record. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=10508" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:08</a> | Are the toledot missing clans, or are the lifespans deflated? | He leans toward genealogical compression or clan structuring more than a simple lifespan-deflation theory. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=10593" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:33</a> | Are there studies of carbon-14 dating being affected by environmental factors? | He says local contamination and reservoir effects can matter, but not in a way that rescues every weak chronology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=10925" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:05</a> | When did the Exodus occur, 1265 or 1260 BC? | He favors a mid-13th-century setting and treats the exact year as less certain than the general window. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11020" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:40</a> | Would a red heifer sacrifice usher in the antichrist? | He says it may be needed for a restored holy place, but it would not necessarily trigger anything immediately. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11096" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:56</a> | How would you describe the Trinity to a new Christian or a mild skeptic? | He explains one God in three persons, with real distinction but shared divine essence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11224" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:07:04</a> | If you had 40 years to start and complete a kingdom project, what would you do? | He says he does not plan 40 years ahead and prefers smaller projects he can finish within a shorter horizon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11452" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:52</a> | Why are usernames showing up instead of display names? | He says it is part of YouTube's top-fans and experience-points system. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:18</a> | What is the point of YouTube experience points, and does it benefit viewers? | He treats it as platform gamification meant to encourage more super chats, and says it does not benefit viewers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:16</a> | What is a VTuber? | He explains it as a performer using an animated avatar instead of live face video. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=11810" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:50</a> | How should we weigh Williamson's new Exodus commentary alongside the bigger early-date and late-date evidence cluster? | He says the evidence has to be weighed cumulatively, because no single verse, site, or commentator settles the date by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=12036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:36</a> | Rhythm or lead guitar, and what is your favorite song to play? | He says guitar is mostly a songwriting tool for him rather than a performance identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=12451" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:31</a> | Should I start an animal-themed Christian meme page, and what animal would you be? | He says yes if it is actually useful and funny, and then answers playfully about the animal angle. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=12586" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:46</a> | How is the plague book coming along? | He says work is paused until the video game is finished and may resume around mid-September. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=12682" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:22</a> | Will the video game be a first-person shooter RPG? | He says no; it is planned as a fantasy-themed visual novel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=12703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:43</a> | Is traditional publishing still worth it versus indie publishing? | He says he asks that every day, because traditional publishing offers little editing or marketing in his experience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=13292" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:32</a> | Do you accept Yurco's interpretation of Merneptah's war reliefs at Karnak, especially Israelites in the fourth scene? | He agrees with Yurco that the fourth, topmost register probably depicts Israelites. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=13351" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:42:31</a> | Did Egyptians, especially Cleopatra, value earthworms for gardening? | He says earthworms were not really a thing in Egypt because the soil and flooding conditions were unsuitable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=13442" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:02</a> | Will the video game have voice actors? | He says no, citing the cost and internationalization problems voice acting would create. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=13947" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:52:27</a> | How do you avoid getting addicted to social media? | He says by limiting exposure, treating it as a tool, and refusing to let it become an ambient habit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=14077" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:37</a> | Did ancient Egypt treat citizens and sojourners alike in law and tradition? | He says Egypt was governed more by order and tradition than law, and free foreigners could be treated similarly under that tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=14249" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:29</a> | Has a beta reader betrayed trust and behaved badly? | He says yes, and explains that public discussion of private beta-reading process details can betray confidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=14611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:31</a> | What if you had a beta reader like from *Misery*? | He says that would not be a beta reader but a fan gone berserk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/u6o1iG0ZuAk?t=14635" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:55</a> | What is *Misery*? | He says it is Stephen King's novel, referenced in passing. |
